mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL signals persistent inflation that erodes real earnings and compresses valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers recessionary discount rate adjustments and risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger fires during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a broad demand-supply contraction that amplifies earnings downgrade risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals aggressive monetary tightening that rapidly raises equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month while CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving capacity utilization and operating leverage that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of months when INDPRO rises month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 150 thousand signals robust labor income and consumption capacity that supports top-line revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when PAYEMS rises more than 150k in a month over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a Goldilocks scenario of accommodative policy alongside labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of weeks when both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE decline month-over-month over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) alongside a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a consumer-led recessionary dynamic that triggers broad-based de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of quarters when RSAFS declines month-over-month while UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.
